meaning: n.出售麦酒(比普通啤酒要烈,色深)的酒店,酒馆
examples: We had an alehouse boy who attended always in the house to supply the workmen.
The alehouse became a problem of widespread concern in the 16 ~ th and 17 ~ th century England.
At which point another habitue of the alehouse leant over and pointed out (he being a computing expert) that the memory required would simply be vastly expensive.
